nodes.context: Splinter remnant of a bandit and mercenary group recruited from Zhuang and former Taiping soldiers, active in northern Vietnam and China.
Rebellion in China that was crushed in 1864; former soldiers joined the Black Flag Army.
A band of former Taiping rebels who settled to peaceful pursuits and were subsidized by the Anamese to check the Black Flags.
Another bandit group fought by the Black Flag Army.
Splinter remnant of bandit and mercenary group recruited from Zhuang soldiers and former Taiping soldiers, active in northern Vietnam against French forces.
Vietnamese resistance movement against French colonial rule.

edges.evidence_quote: The Black Flag Army ... was a splinter remnant of a bandit and mercenary group recruited largely from soldiers of ethnic Zhuang background and former Taiping soldiers
edges.context: The Black Flag Army was formed from remnants of the Taiping forces.

edges.evidence_quote: The Anamese subsidized the Yellow Flags partly to act as a check upon the troublesome Black Flags
edges.context: The Yellow Flag Army was used by the Anamese as a check against the Black Flag Army, indicating rivalry.

edges.evidence_quote: During this period, the Black Flag Army also successfully fought other groups of bandits and mercenaries, such as the White Flag Army and Yellow Flag Army.
edges.context: The Black Flag Army fought against the White Flag Army.

edges.evidence_quote: most of the unwanted Black Flag soldiers immediately took to banditry, under cover of the Cần Vương resistance movement against the French.
edges.context: Black Flag soldiers used the Cần Vương movement as cover for banditry.
